mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) are a proxy for consumer spending. Rising retail sales indicate strengthening demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward quintile spread of 20-day returns between high and low RSAFS growth periods is not positive with t-stat > 2.0 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions. Higher unemployment reduces disposable income and consumption, leading to negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the news.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: In months following an UNRATE increase of 0.3pp or more, average 5-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The real interest rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation from CPIAUCSL) measures the opportunity cost of equity investing. Negative real rates make equities more attractive relative to cash, driving positive forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: low nominal rate environment with moderate inflation
failure_condition: When trailing real rate is negative, the average 20-day SPX return is not greater than when real rate is non-negative (one-sided test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a leading indicator of economic activity. In a post-recession recovery regime, rising INDPRO signals accelerating output and improving corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-recession periods (defined as NBER trough to 24 months after), the rank correlation between 3-month change in INDPRO and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer confidence. A sustained rise in HOUST above a key threshold (500,000 units) signals a housing market recovery, which positively impacts equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 500,000 units in a month
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return following months where HOUST exceeds 500,000 is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in both employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a robust expansion with rising consumer incomes and spending. The joint signal confirms positive demand momentum, driving S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS have positive month-over-month growth, the average 20-day SPX return is not greater than the average return when either is negative (one-sided test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (ZLB), the central bank cannot ease further in response to negative shocks. This policy impotence raises the probability of adverse macroeconomic outcomes, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or is positive, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic output. A positive quarterly change in GDPC1 signals accelerating economic growth, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over any 15-year rolling window.
